channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump withdraws Rep. Elise Stefanik's nomination for UN ambassador, citing tight GOP House margin
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced Thursday that he was pulling Rep. Elise Stefanik's nomination to be U.S.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Civil rights groups say push to dismantle Education Department will undue hard-won gains
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The rights of Americans to self-determine how to educate their children — a hotly contested matter that stretches back to at least the Civil War — have long been intertwined with the principle of equally and equitably educating children across racial lines.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mixed verdict for 3 Philadelphia detectives in perjury trial involving a 2016 murder exoneration
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The trial of three long-retired Philadelphia detectives accused of lying about evidence in a homicide case that later ended with an exoneration wrapped up with a mixed verdict Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio says at least 300 foreign students' visas have been revoked
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Thursday at least 300 foreign students have had their visas revoked amid the Trump administration's immigration agenda, a far higher number than was previously known.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin says US push for Greenland rooted in history, vows to uphold Russian interest in the Arctic
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia's President Vladimir Putin said Thursday that President Donald Trump's push for control over Greenland wasn't surprising given longtime U.S. interest in the mineral-rich territory.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Health and Human Services will lay off 10,000 workers in major restructuring
item.description: In a major overhaul, the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services will lay off 10,000 workers and shut down entire agencies, including ones that oversee billions of dollars in funds for addiction services and community health centers across the country.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FAA acknowledges it must do better after deadly DC air collision
item.description: The head of the Federal Aviation Administration told Congress during a hearing Thursday about a midair collision over Washington, D.C., that killed 67 people that the agency must do more to ensure flying remains safe.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man arrested in Molotov cocktail attack at Tesla shop in Las Vegas will also face federal charges
item.description: Las Vegas police on Thursday said a man suspected of attacking a Tesla location with Molotov cocktails and damaging at least five vehicles has been arrested.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University is latest Palestinian supporter swept up in US crackdown
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Turkish student detained by federal officers as she walked along a street in a Boston suburb is the latest supporter of Palestinian causes to be swept up in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants who have expressed their political views.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Meghan Markle wants royals to ‘cave,' using royal title to make money: Insider
item.description: Meghan Markle's rep didn't return an email asking if the As Ever or ShopMy proceeds were going to charity or her account.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Sundance Film Festival is leaving Utah for Colorado, starting in 2027
item.description: Boulder has officially been unveiled as the city to host the Sundance Film Festival starting in 2027, according to a press release from the Sundance Institute.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, KREX 5

item.title: Toddler sneaks onto White House grounds
item.description: A toddler slipped through the White House fence on Wednesday, the Secret Service said in a post on the social platform X. “Just after 6:30 p.m. on Wednesday, U.S. Secret Service Uniformed Division officers observed a child slip through the White House north fence.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KTLA 5

item.title: Watch live: Senate holds hearing on rising antisemitism on college campuses
item.description: The Senate Health, Education, Labor and Pensions Committee is holding a hearing on the perceived rise of antisemitism on college campuses.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Asian Americans and Pacific Islanders are still targets of hatred 5 years after pandemic surge
item.description: Up until 2020, Anna Wong had gone her entire adulthood in Los Angeles without ever facing blatantly racist abuse for being Asian. After COVID-19 hit, she was accosted twice in six months.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alleged leader of MS-13 street gang on the East Coast is arrested in Virginia
item.description: U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi says the alleged leader of the MS-13 street gang on the East Coast has been arrested in Virginia.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tourist submarine sinks near Egypt, leaving 6 dead and 9 inured, officials say
item.description: The submarine, which was sailing off one of the beaches in the tourist promenade area, had carried 45 tourists of various nationalities.
item.dc:creator: Samy Magdy, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: First job? Some tax tips for first-time filers
item.description: The deadline to file your taxes is less than a month away, and if you're doing them for the first time, you might be feeling added pressure.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How a 1970s Chicano art group defied the mainstream and made history
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — When filmmaker Travis Gutiérrez Senger reflects on ASCO’s legacy, he quickly notes they were more than an art group; they created a movement, one with remarkable influence on Chicano art history. “That movement continues today, and it’s very expansive,” he says.
item.dc:creator: LESLIE AMBRIZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chance of Laken Riley's illegal immigrant killer doing time in El Salvador prison addressed by lawmakers
item.description: Could Jose Ibarra, Laken Riley's convicted killer, be deported to an El Salvador prison where the Trump administration just sent hundreds of Venezuelan gang members?
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Firefighters make progress on containing some Carolina wildfires, but others grow
item.description: Firefighters announced progress Wednesday night on containing two of the largest wildfires burning in the North Carolina mountains, but they warned that fire danger remained from dry and windy conditions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Veterans organization gives back to severely wounded 'appreciative' Ukrainian soldiers
item.description: Ret. Air Force Lt. Col. Jim Lorraine spoke to Fox News Digital about his nonprofit's efforts to aid wounded Ukrainian soldiers in the ongoing war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday on Capitol Hill over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KRON4

item.title: Chewing gum releases thousands of microplastics into body, study finds
item.description: A recent study found that chewing gum can release up to thousands of microplastics per piece and potentially be ingested, according to researchers.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: More Americans say NPR, PBS should be federally funded than not: Survey
item.description: The same survey found 32 percent of Democrats say they regularly get news from NPR, vs. 9 percent of Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Cartel 'sicarios' crossed into US soil in murder-for-hire scheme, prosecutors say
item.description: A Mexican cartel leader has been sentenced to U.S. federal prison for a murder-for-hire scheme in which prosecutors say he brought sicarios, or hit men, across the border into Laredo, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Trump places 25% tariff on imported autos, expecting to raise $100 billion in tax revenues
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the tariffs would be detailed at a 4 p.m. EST news conference.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NATO issues new statement on fate of 4 missing Fort Stewart soldiers
item.description: WIESBADEN, Germany (WSAV) — The NATO Acting spokesperson said that the Secretary General was referring to news articles and did not confirm the death of four U.S. Army 3rd Infantry Division soldiers reported missing in Lithuania Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: San Diego passes first-in-the-nation policy to ban digital-only coupons at grocery stores
item.description: The San Diego City Council has passed the Grocery Pricing Transparency Ordinance, requiring grocers to offer both digital and paper coupons to all customers, promoting equal access to grocery savings opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dollar Tree selling Family Dollar to a pair of private equity firms for $1 billion
item.description: Dollar Tree is selling Family Dollar to a pair of private equity firms for $1 billion after unsuccessfully trying to incorporate the bargain chain for a decade.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN AP Business News, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How the Signal 'group chat' furor has affected the 5 biggest players
item.description: The fact that such inherently sensitive information was being communicated in such a forum — and in front of a reporter whom no other participant appears to have noticed — has caused a political storm.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, KRON4

item.title: Wildfires in North and South Carolina fueled by drought, wind and fallen trees from Hurricane Helene
item.description: Dry conditions, wind and trees downed by Hurricane Helene fueled wildfires in North Carolina and South Carolina, where evacuation orders were in effect Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Boston skating community hosts the world championships even as it mourns plane crash victims
item.description: NORWOOD, Mass. (AP) — The chairs where Jinna Han and Spencer Lane used to sit are still there, and covered with gifts: cards and cookies, plants and pictures — anything that might have sparked a connection for their friends and fellow skaters.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fired federal workers face choices now that a judge wants them rehired
item.description: Sam Peterson is one of thousands of fired federal workers who was offered his job back under a judge’s order, but he didn’t jump at the chance to go back to his park ranger position at Washington state’s Lake Roosevelt National Recreation Area.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jeffries calls for investigation of top Trump officials after bombshell military breach
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) is calling for Congress to investigate top Trump administration officials in the wake of a bombshell revelation that The Atlantic’s top editor was included in a group chat discussion detailing the Pentagon’s plans to strike Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WGN 9

item.title: Atlantic journalist says Trump officials included him in war plans group chat
item.description: Jeffrey Goldberg, editor in chief of The Atlantic, was apparently included in a Trump administration group chat on Signal in which top officials debated and then discussed details of attacks against Houthi rebels in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to intervene in rehiring of federal workers
item.description: The Trump administration Monday asked the Supreme Court to block an order forcing the government to rehire thousands of workers fired as part of President Trump’s effort to drastically reduce the size of the federal workforce. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: 3 killed and 15 hurt in a shooting at a park in New Mexico’s Las Cruces
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — Three people were fatally shot, and 15 others were hurt, after an altercation broke out at a park in the desert city of Las Cruces, police said Saturday. Police and fire crews arrived at just after 10 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH and MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, WGN 9
